title: Qualys - Broader Module Capability Map Beyond VMDR
applies_to: Choosing the correct Qualys module or API family for a requested extraction task
Separating VMDR, WAS, reporting, and asset-centric workflows

confirmed_facts.fact: Classic VM APIs expose host detections and report-management APIs, including list reports, launch report, launch report using asset tags, fetch report, and run asset search report.
The Host Detection List API in the VM family is the source of current host vulnerability state and history for VMDR-style metrics.
Global IT Asset Inventory v2 is a gateway API that provides asset-centric data such as names, addresses, operating-system details, tags, and hostId or assetId values.
WAS exposes searchable findings, web applications, scans, downloadable scan results, and downloadable reports through its own QPS routes.
The WAS UI documents API assets with tags, open vulnerabilities, and TruRisk score, meaning API-security-oriented reporting can be rooted in WAS rather than VMDR.

module_selection_rules: Use classic VM or VMDR host detections when the question is about host vulnerabilities, host severity counts, host status history, or tag-scoped host vulnerability metrics.
Use Global IT Asset Inventory when the question is about asset identity, tag membership, OS inventory, asset grouping, or joining host vulnerability facts to richer asset metadata.
Use classic VM report APIs when the request explicitly asks for Qualys-generated report artifacts or report schedules rather than raw rows.
Use WAS findings and WAS reports when the request is about web applications, API assets, application findings, URLs, web-app tags, or API-security-style vulnerability metrics.

Do not assume a single universal Qualys vulnerability schema. VMDR host detections and WAS findings are different reporting grains and should stay separate unless the consumer explicitly wants a cross-module aggregate.
implementation_notes: This entry is a routing aid for future research and generation tasks. It tells the engine which Qualys API family is likely relevant before deeper extraction begins.
Asset data exists in more than one Qualys context. Global IT Asset Inventory is the broad host or device inventory surface, while WAS API assets are application or API-scan objects.
When a request says only 'Qualys report', the engine should clarify or infer whether the target is host security, asset inventory, or application security before choosing endpoints.
caveats: The reviewed docs are enough to map the major module families, but they do not exhaust every Qualys product surface.
Broader modules such as cloud or container products may also be extractable, but they were not the focus of this pass and should be researched separately if needed.
